etymology.description: Four parallel 一(一) (yī, "one") = four lines -> four
etymology.interpretation: The character 亖 is an ideogram depicting four horizontal lines, meant to represent the number four by repeating the single line 一(一) (yī, "one") four times. This representation was eventually replaced by 四(四) (sì, "four") for clarity in writing systems, particularly to avoid confusion with 二(二) (èr, "two") and 三(三) (sān, "three") in vertical scripts.
